title: TIS 1099

infobox.value: (short **TIS 1099**) is a national standard assigning numerical codes to the [administrative subdivisions](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Thailand "Subdivisions of Thailand") of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), published in 2005 by the [Thai Industrial Standards Institute](/w/index.php?title=Thai_Industrial_Standards_Institute&action=edit&redlink=1 "Thai Industrial Standards Institute (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["ประกาศกระทรวงอุตสาหกรรม ฉบับที่ ๓๔๖๙ (พ.ศ. ๒๕๔๙) ออกตามความในพระราชบัญญัติมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม พ.ศ. ๒๕๑๑ เรื่อง ยกเลิกมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม รหัสจังหวัดเพื่อการแลกเปลี่ยนข้อมูลและกำหนดมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม รหัสจังหวัด อำเภอ และตำบล เพื่อการแลกเปลี่ยนข้อมูล"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120125101529/http://www.ratchakitcha.soc.go.th/DATA/PDF/2549/D/062/32.PDF) (PDF). *Royal Gazette* (in Thai). **123** (62 ง): 32. 2006-06-29. Archived from [the original](http://www.ratchakitcha.soc.go.th/DATA/PDF/2549/D/062/32.PDF) (PDF) on January 25, 2012.

["ประกาศกระทรวงอุตสาหกรรม ฉบับที่ ๑๘๑๕ (พ.ศ. ๒๕๓๕) ออกตามความในพระราชบัญญัติมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม พ.ศ. ๒๕๑๑ เรื่อง กำหนดมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรมรหัสจังหวัดเพื่อการแลกเปลี่ยนข้อมูล"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120125101305/http://www.ratchakitcha.soc.go.th/DATA/PDF/2535/D/104/9011.PDF) (PDF). *Royal Gazette* (in Thai). **109** (104 ง): 9011. 1992-08-20. Archived from [the original](http://www.ratchakitcha.soc.go.th/DATA/PDF/2535/D/104/9011.PDF) (PDF) on January 25, 2012.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [TIS 1099](/w/index.php?title=TIS_1099&redirect=no "TIS 1099"))

Administrative codes of Thailand

**Thai Industry Standard 1099-2548** (short **TIS 1099**) is a national standard assigning numerical codes to the [administrative subdivisions](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Thailand "Subdivisions of Thailand") of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), published in 2005 by the [Thai Industrial Standards Institute](/w/index.php?title=Thai_Industrial_Standards_Institute&action=edit&redlink=1 "Thai Industrial Standards Institute (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thai_Industrial_Standard_1099-2548&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1992, a first version of the TIS 1099 standard (named TIS 1099-2535) was published,[[2]](#cite_note-2) assigning codes to each of the then 72 provinces and the capital city of Bangkok. It followed a numbering scheme already used within the Ministry of Interior since the 1980s.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The [ISO standard 3199-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2:TH "ISO 3166-2:TH"), first published in 1998, follows the codes of this standard. The draft version of ISO 3166 was based on the US standard [FIPS 10-4](/wiki/FIPS_10-4 "FIPS 10-4"), which assigns different numbers to the provinces. Additionally to the codes for the province and Bangkok, the ISO standard adds a special code for the special administrative area [Pattaya](/wiki/Pattaya "Pattaya"), a code not present in the Thai standard.

In 2005, the current version of the standard was published, introducing the codes for [Amnat Charoen](/wiki/Amnat_Charoen_Province "Amnat Charoen Province"), [Nong Bua Lam Phu](/wiki/Nong_Bua_Lam_Phu_Province "Nong Bua Lam Phu Province") and [Sa Kaeo](/wiki/Sa_Kaeo_Province "Sa Kaeo Province"), created in 1993. It also introduced codes for every [district](/wiki/Amphoe "Amphoe") and [subdistrict](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon"), and some of the [municipalities](/wiki/Thesaban "Thesaban"), especially those already existing before the [sanitary districts](/wiki/Sukhaphiban "Sukhaphiban") were made into municipalities in 1999.

Since the last publication of the standard, the province [Bueng Kan](/wiki/Bueng_Kan_Province "Bueng Kan Province") and the district [Galyani Vadhana](/wiki/Galyani_Vadhana_District "Galyani Vadhana District") as well as some subdistricts in Bangkok were created. Numerical codes for these entities have been assigned by the Ministry of Interior, which continues to publish regular updates to their code list.

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thai_Industrial_Standard_1099-2548&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Structure")]

Every province has a 2 digit numerical code assigned, starting with 10 for Bangkok till 96 for [Narathiwat Province](/wiki/Narathiwat_Province "Narathiwat Province"). However, not all codes in between are used, some are kept for future use. The first digit designates a region, based on the administrative region as they were in use in the 1950s.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The second digit enumerates the provinces within the region. As there are now already 12 provinces in the region 4, the northern [Isan](/wiki/Isan "Isan"), the codes 38 and 39 have been used for the latest provinces in this region.

The districts have a four digit codes, starting with the two digits of the province and the second two digits enumerating the districts within that province. In some cases codes seem to have been omitted, but these actually are the codes of districts which were reassigned to a new province. Also, there were several districts ready to be created in 1998, however the [Asian financial crisis](/wiki/Asian_financial_crisis "Asian financial crisis") put these to halt. Codes for these districts were already assigned. [Wiang Kao District](/wiki/Wiang_Kao_District "Wiang Kao District") created in 2006 then received the code 4029, as 4026 to 4028 were assigned to abandoned district creations. However, when Galyani Vadhana District was created in 2012 it received the code 5025, even though it was in fact one of the abandoned districts of 1997 which would have received the number 5026.

The subdistricts have a six digit code, adding another two digits to the code of the district in which they are located. Again, omitted codes within the enumeration of the subdistrict of one district belong to subdistricts which were reassigned to a different district, usually a newly created district.

The municipalities listed in the standard also have four digit codes like the districts, however starting with 99 and counting down. The provincial capital always has the code ending in 99. For several provinces the number of municipalities is now so high that no longer all can receive a code within the scheme.

While not part of the standard, the Ministry of Interior uses numerical codes for the [administrative villages](/wiki/Muban "Muban") as the lowest subdivision based on the subdistrict codes by adding two digit containing the village number. However, unlike the higher levels of subdivisions, the villages get renumbered if some get reassigned to a different subdistrict, therefore the codes for villages are not stable in time.

### Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thai_Industrial_Standard_1099-2548&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Examples")]

* Bangkok: 10
* [Phra Nakhon District](/wiki/Phra_Nakhon_District "Phra Nakhon District"), Bangkok: 1001
* Wat Sam Phraya Subdistrict, Phra Nakhon, Bangkok: 100112
* [Chiang Mai Province](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_Province "Chiang Mai Province"): 50
* [Chiang Mai City](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai"): 5099
